1.Title: Fluctuating Response Time Under Load
1.Insight: For the endpoint Homepage, response time remained **elevated and unstable** as [requests per second](#ClientMetrics) grew, indicating backend performance variability.
1.Details: [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) held steady at 10 from 11:08 AM UTC to 11:10 AM UTC. [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) peaked at **6.4 RPS at 11:09 AM UTC**, with fluctuations throughout the run. [Response time](#ClientMetrics) dropped from 3590ms at 11:08 AM UTC to 2220ms at 11:08 AM UTC, then fluctuated between 1920ms and 2430ms until 11:10 AM UTC, with a minimum of **1920ms at 11:09 AM UTC**.
1.Recommendation: Consider profiling the server application for bottlenecks and reviewing resource allocation during traffic spikes. Investigate asynchronous processing, database latency, or caching to stabilize and reduce response time.

2.Title: Load Pattern Impact on Throughput
2.Insight: Throughput for [requests per second](#ClientMetrics) rose with constant [virtual user](#ClientMetrics) count but dropped temporarily before recovering, possibly reflecting backend throttling or transient resource limits at peak load for Homepage.
2.Details: [Requests per second](#ClientMetrics) increased from 2.1 RPS at 11:08 AM UTC to **6.4 RPS at 11:09 AM UTC**, then fell to 4.6 RPS at 11:09 AM UTC, rebounded to 5.9 RPS at 11:09 AM UTC and N/A, and ended at 2.9 RPS by 11:10 AM UTC. [Virtual users](#ClientMetrics) remained stable, only dropping from 10 to 8 at the end.
2.Recommendation: May review server-side request handling limits and monitor for any throttling or overload protection mechanisms during peak load intervals. Implement server autoscaling or optimize backend logic for improved throughput consistency.

3.Summary: Load test (2 min, 10 VUs) ran successfully with minor latency spikes. Response times fluctuated between 1.9s and 2.4s with no errors, indicating stable client-side performance under load.
